# Context File Template

Use this template when generating `.claude/prompt-forge-context.md` for a project. Fill it in from your codebase analysis. Every section should contain real data from the actual project — no placeholders left behind.

## Template

```markdown
# Prompt Forge — Project Context
<!-- Auto-generated by Prompt Forge. Edit freely — changes are preserved on updates. -->
<!-- Last updated: [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM] -->

## Project overview
- **Name:** [from package.json name / pyproject.toml / go.mod / etc.]
- **Type:** [API / fullstack / CLI / library / monorepo]
- **Stack:** [e.g., Express 4.18 + TypeScript 5.3 + Prisma 5.10 + PostgreSQL]
- **Runtime:** [e.g., Node 20, Python 3.12, Go 1.22]

## Structure map

### Backend
- `[path]/` — [what this directory contains]
  - `[file]` — [one-line description: key exports or purpose]
  - `[file]` — [description]
[repeat for each meaningful directory]

### Frontend (if applicable)
- `[path]/` — [component directory, pages, etc.]
  - `[file]` — [description]

### Database
- `[schema path]` — Models: [list model names]

### Tests
- `[test dir]/` — [test framework + runner]
  - `[test file]` — ✅ has tests
  - `[expected test file]` — ❌ missing
[mark coverage gaps with ❌ — this directly feeds prompt generation]

### Config
- `[config file]` — [what it configures]
[package.json, tsconfig, .env.example, docker-compose, etc.]

## Patterns catalog

### [Pattern name] pattern
```
[Short description of the pattern flow]
[e.g., Router → Zod validation → middleware → Service → JSON response]
Reference: @[actual file that exemplifies this pattern]
```

### [Pattern name] pattern
```
[Description]
Reference: @[file]
```

[Include patterns for: routes/endpoints, services/business logic, data access,
testing, error handling, validation. Only patterns that actually exist in the codebase.]

## Key paths by domain

### [Domain name, e.g., Auth & identity]
- Route: `[path]`
- Service: `[path]`
- Middleware: `[path]` (if applicable)
- Tests: `[path]` or ❌ none

### [Domain name]
- [relevant files grouped]

[Group files by feature area. This lets the skill immediately find all files
related to a user's request without searching.]

## Commands
- **Test:** `[actual command from package.json scripts]`
- **Lint:** `[actual command]`
- **Typecheck:** `[actual command]`
- **Dev:** `[actual command]`
- **Build:** `[actual command]`
[Only include commands that actually exist in the project]

## Known conventions
- [Patterns the codebase follows consistently]
[e.g., "All routes use Zod for request validation"]
[e.g., "Error responses always use { error: string } format"]
[e.g., "Auth middleware attaches user to req.user as JwtPayload"]
[e.g., "Services are class-based, one class per file"]

## Known gaps & anti-patterns
- [Things that are missing or inconsistent — tech debt, bugs, missing coverage]
[e.g., "No test coverage for auth or user routes"]
[e.g., "loginUser() has inconsistent error handling (null vs throw)"]
[e.g., "getUserProfile() makes 3 sequential DB calls (should be parallelized)"]
[e.g., "Redis is in package.json but no client is configured"]
[This section is gold for prompt generation — it surfaces issues the developer
might not think to mention when they're tired]
```

## Adapting to project type

### Python projects
- Stack from `pyproject.toml`, `requirements.txt`, or `Pipfile`
- Structure: `app/` or `src/` with `routers/`, `services/`, `models/`
- Tests: `pytest` in `tests/` directory
- Commands: `pytest`, `ruff check`, `mypy`

### Go projects
- Stack from `go.mod`
- Structure: `cmd/`, `internal/`, `pkg/`
- Tests: `go test ./...` (tests alongside source as `_test.go`)
- Commands: `go build`, `go test`, `golangci-lint`

### Frontend (React/Next.js/Vue)
- Replace backend sections with:
  - Components directory tree
  - Pages/routes structure
  - State management (Redux, Zustand, Context)
  - API client layer (fetch wrapper, Axios, React Query)
  - Styling approach (Tailwind, CSS Modules, styled-components)
- Tests: Vitest/Jest + React Testing Library

### Monorepo
- Add a top-level workspace map
- Each package/app gets its own structure map section
- Shared packages get their own section
- Note cross-package dependencies

### Fullstack
- Include both Backend and Frontend sections
- Note the boundary (API contract, shared types, etc.)
- Map which frontend components consume which API endpoints

## Rules for generating

1. **Only include what actually exists.** Don't add placeholder sections for things the project doesn't have.
2. **Use ❌ to mark gaps.** Missing test files, unconfigured tools, unused dependencies — these are valuable for prompt generation.
3. **Reference files are real paths.** Every `@path` must be a real file.
4. **Keep it scannable.** This file will be read by an LLM on every invocation. Short lines, consistent format, no prose paragraphs.
5. **Update, don't regenerate.** When updating stale sections, preserve the rest. The developer may have edited the conventions or gaps sections manually.

## Growing sections (added over time)

These sections don't exist on first generation. They grow as the skill learns across sessions.

### Prompt Forge notes
Tracks how to prompt for this specific developer on this specific project. See SKILL.md Step 7D for what goes here.

```markdown
## Prompt Forge notes

### Developer preferences (prompt-internal — NOT for CLAUDE.md)
- [Prompt style preferences]
- [Which lenses matter most]
- [Common adjustments made to generated prompts]

### What works for this project
- [Task-type-specific prompt patterns that produce good results]
```

### Learned approaches
A growing library of principles extracted from tools, frameworks, and methodologies the developer has shared or that Prompt Forge has discovered. Each entry captures the **core principle** — not the tool itself, but the thinking pattern that's reusable.

```markdown
## Learned approaches

### Self-learning loop (from Hermes Agent)
- Core: capture successful workflows as reusable documents, improve them during use
- Applied: CLAUDE.md evolves with every session, context file tracks what works
- Source: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com

### Progressive skill loading (from DeerFlow)
- Core: load only what's needed, when it's needed — don't front-load everything
- Applied: context file lets Prompt Forge skip broad analysis on repeat sessions
- Source: https://deerflow.tech

### [Principle name] (from [tool/methodology])
- Core: [one-line principle]
- Applied: [how it's used in this project's prompting/development]
- Source: [URL or reference]
```

This section is the skill's expanding knowledge base. Every time the developer shares a tool or Prompt Forge discovers a novel approach during research, extract the principle and add it here. Future sessions can draw from this library to propose approaches the developer might not think of.
